taxonomy: Amphilophis radicans is classified as a species of plant in the family Orobanchaceae. This family is commonly known as the broomrape family. The Orobanchaceae family consists of parasitic plants that derive their nutrients from other plants. Amphilophis radicans is native to Central and South America. This plant is a type of hemiparasite, which means it obtains some of its nutrients from the soil but also derives nutrients from other plants.
